cells.2: ⚠ NON-MORALIZATION DISCLAIMER

This diagnostic does not rank people, functions, or types by human worth. It evaluates the fit between observed decision patterns and a specific M&A integration problem. Environment names are operational classifiers describing how an organisation's decision logic and resource allocation function — they are not moral judgments about the people who work in those organisations.
STRUCTURAL TYPOLOGY · Free-Tier Output Narratives · 72 Pair-Specific Client-Facing Outputs

One narrative per acquirer-environment × target-environment pair. Delivered automatically when both environment codes are confirmed from the diagnostic instruments. Designed for cold-traffic M&A professionals: PE partners, VC directors, M&A advisors.
NARRATIVE PURPOSE — WHAT EACH OUTPUT MUST ACCOMPLISH
Confirm the diagnosis
cells.3: The narrative opens by naming the pair combination in plain language — not in framework terms. The client must feel that the instrument understood what their organisation is, not that it assigned them a code.

cells.2: Frame the risk they already believe
cells.3: The situation paragraph does not introduce new concepts. It names the specific tension that PE and M&A professionals already know exists — in the language they already use — and shows that the framework has a structural explanation for it.

cells.2: Give one verifiable prediction
cells.3: The prediction paragraph gives one specific, observable early-warning signal the client can check within 30 days of close. It is not a general risk statement. It is a falsifiable prediction. If it is correct, the framework has earned credibility. If it is incorrect, the client has learned something useful about their situation.

cells.2: Position the paid path without selling it
cells.3: The CTA is identical across all 72 narratives and is framed as a logical consequence of the prediction — 'if this signal appears, here is what the full analysis gives you.' It does not ask the client to buy anything. It tells them what is available.

cells.2: NARRATIVE STRUCTURE — IDENTICAL ACROSS ALL 72 PAIRS
HEADLINE (bold)
cells.3: One line. What the pair combination signals in plain language.

cells.2: SITUATION (2-3 sentences)
cells.3: What the diagnostic result means for this deal. Uses second person ('your target', 'your deal'). No framework jargon. References specific timelines (months 6-18, days 30-60).

cells.2: PREDICTION (1 sentence)
cells.3: One specific, observable, falsifiable early-warning signal. Always begins 'Within 30 days of close:' or 'Within 30 days:'. Derived from the Early Warning column of ST_Friction_Point_Lookup.xlsx.

cells.2: IMPLICATION (1 sentence)
cells.3: What happens if the prediction signal appears — the FP3 consequence with a specific timeline. Grounds the prediction in a concrete outcome.

cells.2: CTA (standard)
cells.3: Identical across all 72 pairs. Positions the paid engagement as the logical next step. Never says 'buy' or 'purchase' or 'engage us'.

cells.2: BAND CALIBRATION — HOW TONE SHIFTS BY ECS SCORE
HIGH COMPATIBILITY (80-100)
cells.3: Acknowledge residual friction explicitly — do not dismiss it. The client is looking for confirmation that the deal is safe. Give them the confirmation, then name the one friction point that is not obvious. Tone: confident, precise, reassuring without being uncritical.

cells.2: MODERATE-HIGH (65-79)
cells.3: Name the tension clearly and specifically. Position verification as useful rather than urgent. Tone: direct, informative, constructive — this is manageable with the right information.

cells.2: MODERATE (50-64)
cells.3: Frame as requiring active monitoring rather than immediate intervention. The prediction is the most important element — give the client something to watch. Tone: measured, specific, not alarming but not dismissive.

cells.2: MODERATE-LOW (35-49)
cells.3: Frame as requiring structural intervention pre-close, not post-close monitoring. The situation paragraph is longer and more specific about the mechanism. Tone: direct, slightly urgent, evidence-grounded.

cells.2: HIGH RISK (0-34)
cells.3: Frame as a fundamental structural incompatibility. Do not soften. The client needs to know before they sign whether this deal can work. Tone: clear, unambiguous, specific about what goes wrong and when.

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Knowledge generation versus empirical immediacy. NF/NT produces theoretical knowledge as its primary output — slowly, through dialectical convergence. NT/STP needs that knowledge applied to physical tests immediately. NF/NT acquirers attempting to slow NT/STP targets to NF/NT timelines find active resistance: NT/STP participants do not recognise theoretical elaboration as productive work until it can be tested.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Argument quality versus test result authority. NF/NT grants authority to the most precise argument. NT/STP grants authority to whatever the live test demonstrates. These can coexist when the theoretical position and the test result agree. When they diverge — which is structurally inevitable — neither mechanism recognises the other as legitimate.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Application gap at scale. NF/NT environments produce ideas for intrinsic value; application is a downstream consequence. NT/STP is the natural application layer for NF/NT ideas. Under NF/NT governance, NT/STP targets lose the operational urgency that made them effective application vehicles. High-performing NT/STP practitioners who joined because they get to test things immediately find the pace intolerably slow.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/NT management introduces any theoretical elaboration requirement before NT/STP experiments can proceed. Any gate between idea and test that is epistemic rather than resource-based signals that NF/NT timelines are being imposed on NT/STP operational tempo.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NF/NT vs ~NT/STP), Decisiveness (~NF/NT vs +NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Knowledge sharing versus competitive hoarding. NF/NT treats knowledge as a shared good — discoveries are communicated broadly. NT/STJ treats knowledge as a competitive asset managed strategically. NF/NT acquirers attempting to open NT/STJ target knowledge to broader sharing encounter active institutional resistance — NT/STJ's competitive infrastructure depends on information asymmetry.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Argument quality versus track record authority. NF/NT grants authority to the most precise argument regardless of title or track record. NT/STJ grants authority to those with the strongest measured results in the domain. NF/NT management that overrides NT/STJ track-record authority with argument quality is experienced by NT/STJ participants as structurally illegitimate — intellectually interesting but not operationally authoritative.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Speed versus epistemic quality in commercial context. NT/STJ's competitive efficiency operates on faster timelines than NF/NT's dialectical convergence. NF/NT acquirers slow commercial decision cycles to allow for epistemically complete argument. NT/STJ targets experience this as competitive vulnerability — by the time NF/NT finishes its argument process, the commercial opportunity has passed.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NT/STJ target managers continue their performance documentation and competitive positioning practices under NF/NT governance, or whether they begin redirecting energy toward the NF/NT intellectual activity the acquirer rewards. Continuation of competitive documentation indicates NT/STJ logic is still active.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Information (+NF/NT vs +NT/STJ — different flow logic), Knowledge (+NF/NT vs +NT/STJ — shared vs competitive)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Rigour versus resonance decision authority. NF/NT decisions converge through the best argument. NF/SFP decisions emerge through collective feeling-resonance. NF/NT management attempting to introduce analytical rigour into NF/SFP decision processes finds that the argument quality mechanism is simply not accepted as legitimate. NF/SFP participants do not reject the analysis — they find it irrelevant to how they actually decide.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability introduction. NF/SFP has no accountability mechanism. NF/NT introduces argument quality as an accountability criterion — if your position cannot withstand rigorous challenge, it loses authority. NF/SFP creative personnel experience this as a suppression of authentic creative expression in favour of intellectual performance.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Knowledge depth versus aesthetic expression. NF/NT's primary resource is theoretical knowledge with genuine explanatory power. NF/SFP's primary resource is authentic creative expression. These can coexist in individuals — many NF/SFP participants have NF/NT potential. But as institutional logics, they compete for the same cognitive and temporal resources. Under prolonged NF/NT governance, NF/SFP's aesthetic output progressively substitutes intellectual content for visceral authenticity.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce a rigorous analytical critique of an NF/SFP creative output — not hostile, but precise. Observe whether the response is intellectual engagement or emotional withdrawal. Emotional withdrawal (experienced as analytical critique being 'not the point') confirms that NF/SFP resonance-authority is still the operative mechanism.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NF/NT vs −NF/SFP), Money (~NF/NT vs ~NF/SFP), Decisiveness (~NF/NT vs ~NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Innovation framing conflict. SFJ/SFP accepts change only when framed as an extension of tradition. NF/NT introduces change through rigorous argument that tradition is factually incorrect. NF/NT acquirers find that their analytical case for change is structurally irrelevant to SFJ/SFP participants, who evaluate proposals not by their evidential quality but by their continuity with established relational norms.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Cognitive entry requirement versus relational inclusion. NF/NT requires formal operations stage II cognitive development as a practical prerequisite for participation. SFJ/SFP's inclusion criterion is relational fit — have you been here long enough to be trusted? These selection mechanisms produce fundamentally different talent pools, and NF/NT's cognitive standards are experienced by SFJ/SFP participants as exclusion rather than quality control.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Knowledge conservation versus generation. SFJ/SFP conserves accumulated resources within its relational network — knowledge is held by tenured insiders and not freely shared outside. NF/NT requires free knowledge exchange as its core operating mechanism. The tension between SFJ/SFP's conservation instinct and NF/NT's generation-and-sharing ethic progressively reduces knowledge flow, which is the resource NF/NT depends on.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce an NF/NT participant — someone outside the relational network — into a key knowledge exchange. Observe whether SFJ/SFP participants share freely or selectively. Selective sharing with long-tenure insiders but opacity with outsiders (including NF/NT management) is the direct signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NF/NT vs −SFJ/SFP), Information (+NF/NT vs ~SFJ/SFP), Creativity (+NF/NT vs −SFJ/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Argument quality authority in a force-based system. NF/NT authority belongs to the most precise argument. STJ/STP authority belongs to whoever projects the most strength. NF/NT management attempting to exercise authority through analytical precision is experienced by STJ/STP participants as a display of weakness — precision is irrelevant if it cannot be backed by force.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Information openness versus tactical opacity. NF/NT's knowledge-sharing ethic requires transparent information flows. STJ/STP derives competitive advantage from information asymmetry — control over what others know is a primary power mechanism. NF/NT's attempt to open information flows is actively resisted as a power threat.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Ethical framework installation in a coercive environment. STJ/STP has no ethical constraint — the only rule is effective force. NF/NT's ethical framework is among the most developed of all environments. Installing NF/NT ethical governance in STJ/STP without a structural power mechanism to enforce it produces the worst outcome: the appearance of ethical compliance with the continued operation of coercive redistribution underneath.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP managers continue to use information asymmetry — selectively sharing or withholding information — as a management tool under NF/NT ownership. If yes, the STJ/STP power mechanism is still the operative logic regardless of NF/NT's formal governance.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+NF/NT vs −STJ/STP), Knowledge (+NF/NT vs −STJ/STP), Energy (+NF/NT vs −STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Evidence challenge of sacred doctrine. NF/NT's primary mechanism is the revision of beliefs based on better evidence. NF/SFJ's primary mechanism is the protection of beliefs through authority and heresy suppression. When NF/NT acquirers attempt to apply evidence-based evaluation to NF/SFJ target beliefs, the response is institutional — the evaluation method itself is rejected as incompatible with the sacred framework.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Argument quality versus narrative authority. NF/NT: best argument wins. NF/SFJ: most mission-aligned voice wins. NF/NT management cannot establish legitimate authority in NF/SFJ terms — analytic precision is not the currency NF/SFJ recognises. NF/SFJ target leadership retains real authority because they control the narrative, while NF/NT acquirer management holds only formal authority.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Meaning generation versus meaning extraction. NF/NT generates meaning through rigorous inquiry and genuine discovery. NF/SFJ extracts meaning from participants by converting it into a controlled resource. The integration of these two mechanisms produces one of the most destructive environment combinations in the matrix — NF/NT's generative capacity is gradually captured by NF/SFJ's extraction mechanism, converting knowledge creation into doctrine.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce a substantive analytical challenge to one NF/SFJ belief. Measure whether the response is 'let us examine the evidence' or 'that misunderstands what we are doing here.' The latter response, especially if delivered by someone with narrative authority rather than analytical authority, confirms the NF/SFJ structure is intact.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Energy (+NF/NT vs −NF/SFJ), Attention (+NF/NT vs −NF/SFJ), Information (+NF/NT vs −NF/SFJ), Trust (+NF/NT vs −NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Truth versus engineered desire. NF/NT treats truth as the only legitimate currency — all claims must be evidentially justified. SFP/SFJ operates through engineered desire — the system works precisely because participants believe things that are constructed rather than true. NF/NT management's analytical transparency dismantles the belief architecture that makes SFP/SFJ function.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Knowledge generation versus knowledge suppression. NF/NT generates and shares knowledge as a moral imperative. SFP/SFJ suppresses knowledge — particularly about the extraction mechanism — as an operational requirement. Employees who understand how SFP/SFJ works are less controllable. NF/NT's transparency creates exactly this problem at scale.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Talent profile incompatibility at depth. NF/NT requires the highest cognitive development threshold of all environments. SFP/SFJ is optimised for high throughput and low individual cognitive investment. By Month 9, NF/NT management attempting to operate an SFP/SFJ organisation has lost the SFP/SFJ operational expertise that made the target profitable and has not yet established the NF/NT talent base required to replace it.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: share a piece of accurate, transparent operational information with SFP/SFJ employees that the previous regime would have managed or filtered. Observe the response. SFP/SFJ employees who have been systematically exposed to engineered information may not know how to process unmanaged transparency — the confusion response is diagnostic.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+NF/NT vs −SFP/SFJ), Knowledge (+NF/NT vs −SFP/SFJ), Energy (+NF/NT vs ~SFP/SFJ), Information (+NF/NT vs −SFP/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Sacred narrative aligning with commercial extraction. NF/SFJ and SFP/SFJ both extract resources through belief management — NF/SFJ through sacred meaning, SFP/SFJ through engineered desire. The ECS score of 88.2 reflects this structural alignment. The integration friction is minimal at the level of mechanism, but significant at the level of narrative: NF/SFJ requires participants to believe in something transcendent; SFP/SFJ requires them to desire something commercial.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Mission versus consumption as the belief object. The compatibility between NF/SFJ and SFP/SFJ is highest of any pair in the matrix precisely because both manipulate belief. The friction appears when the objects of belief come into conflict — when participants are asked to simultaneously hold the SFP/SFJ consumption belief ('you deserve this now') and the NF/SFJ mission belief ('we are building something sacred'). These are not inherently incompatible but become so when resources are scarce.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Elite extraction alignment. Both NF/SFJ and SFP/SFJ extract resources upward through the hierarchy. The integration produces an environment where the extraction mechanism operates at two levels: ideological extraction (NF/SFJ) and commercial extraction (SFP/SFJ). Participants at lower levels experience a doubling of extraction pressure that eventually overcomes even strong belief-retention.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ's commercial consumption messaging is integrated with NF/SFJ's mission narrative or kept separate. Integration of the two belief systems into a single narrative is the signal that both extraction mechanisms are being combined.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−NF/SFJ vs −SFP/SFJ — both suppress)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authentic community versus mission instrumentalisation. SFJ/SFP provides genuine relational care — unconditional belonging. NF/SFJ provides conditional belonging gated by ideological compliance — the warmth is real but contingent on mission alignment. The initial signal is indistinguishable. The divergence appears at the first test of unconditional care: does NF/SFJ protect someone who challenges the mission? It does not.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Belonging replacement by belief. SFJ/SFP's retention mechanism is genuine relational bonds. NF/SFJ's retention mechanism is belief — people stay because they believe, not because they belong. NF/SFJ governance progressively replaces SFJ/SFP's relational belonging with ideological belief as the primary retention mechanism. Those who cannot believe are no longer retained regardless of relationship depth.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Knowledge preservation within community versus doctrinal control. SFJ/SFP conserves knowledge within its relational network. NF/SFJ controls knowledge to maintain doctrinal authority. The transition from SFJ/SFP to NF/SFJ governance converts the trusted insider network from a knowledge preservation community into a doctrinal compliance community — a transition documented in the project corpus as 'more dangerous for regression than STJ/STP to NT/STJ.'
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ acquirer management maintains SFJ/SFP's unconditional care language while simultaneously introducing mission-compliance requirements. Language continuity masking structural change is NF/SFJ's most reliable early integration pattern.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−NF/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP), Energy (−NF/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP), Connections (~NF/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Mission framing of coercive behaviour. NF/SFJ can absorb STJ/STP's coercive extraction mechanism by providing it with a sacred narrative — the extraction is reframed as service to the mission. This produces a highly stable and dangerous hybrid: STJ/STP coercion operating under NF/SFJ legitimacy. The ECS score of 38.2 reflects this as moderately compatible because both are extraction environments.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Belief versus force retention hybrid. STJ/STP retains through fear. NF/SFJ retains through belief. The combination produces a layered retention system: fear at the operational level, belief at the ideological level. This is more robust than either mechanism alone — people who lose faith are still held by fear, and people who resist force are captured by belief.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Internal legitimacy competition. Over time, STJ/STP's force mechanism and NF/SFJ's belief mechanism compete for authority primacy. In stable conditions, NF/SFJ's narrative provides legitimacy for STJ/STP's force. Under stress, the force mechanism reasserts primacy and the narrative erodes. The organisation oscillates between periods of genuine ideological coherence and periods of naked coercion.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP operational managers adopt the NF/SFJ mission language in their communications, or continue using force-based authority language without the narrative wrapper. Adoption of mission language signals that NF/SFJ legitimation is being installed over STJ/STP coercion.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−NF/SFJ vs −STJ/STP — both suppress)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Doctrinal control versus authentic feeling. NF/SFP's only authority criterion is authentic emotional truth — creative work that genuinely resonates. NF/SFJ converts authentic feeling into controlled doctrine. When NF/SFJ acquirers frame NF/SFP's creative output through their mission narrative, NF/SFP participants experience this as the corruption of the only thing their environment values.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Heresy mechanism versus authentic dissent. NF/SFP resolves conflict through authentic emotional processing — felt and integrated, not suppressed. NF/SFJ suppresses dissent through the heresy mechanism — repositioning challengers as lacking faith. NF/SFJ governance of NF/SFP replaces authentic emotional processing with suppression, destroying the only conflict resolution mechanism that NF/SFP has.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Creative community conversion into ideological vehicle. NF/SFP's creative output is authentic and free. NF/SFJ's purpose is to convert creative and intellectual products into doctrine that serves the elite's extraction mechanism. NF/SFJ acquirers systematically convert NF/SFP's creative community into a content production engine for the NF/SFJ narrative, eliminating authentic creative freedom.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ management attempts to frame NF/SFP creative output within their mission narrative — attributing meaning to work that NF/SFP did not intend. NF/SFP's response to having meaning assigned from outside rather than generated from inside is the diagnostic signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Energy (−NF/SFJ vs +NF/SFP), Trust (−NF/SFJ vs +NF/SFP), Creativity (−NF/SFJ vs +NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Empirical test versus doctrinal mandate. NT/STP resolves every question by running a live test. NF/SFJ resolves every question by reference to the sacred narrative. NF/SFJ acquirers cannot permit tests that might produce results contradicting the mission — every NT/STP experiment is a potential heresy. NT/STP's core operating mechanism is structurally prohibited.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Mission compliance versus technical credibility. NT/STP's authority belongs to those who can actually do the thing. NF/SFJ's authority belongs to those most closely associated with the founding vision. These criteria select for different people. NF/SFJ governance installed in NT/STP displaces technical credibility with mission alignment, producing an organisation that talks like NT/STP but selects like NF/SFJ.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Innovation extinction. NT/STP exists specifically to generate and test innovative solutions — it is the environment's core purpose. NF/SFJ treats innovation as a threat to the established narrative unless it can be framed as doctrinal extension. The combination eliminates NT/STP's innovation capacity within 12 months: nothing can be tested that might challenge the narrative, which is everything interesting.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NT/STP's experiment proposals require mission-alignment approval before execution. Any gate between 'idea' and 'test' that is narrative-based rather than resource-based signals NF/SFJ governance suppressing NT/STP's empirical mechanism.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Energy (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STP), Information (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STP), Creativity (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STP), Knowledge (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Heresy mechanism applied to evidence. NF/NT requires the free revision of beliefs based on better evidence. NF/SFJ requires the protection of beliefs through authority and doctrinal compliance. NF/SFJ acquirers attempting to govern NF/NT targets face an immediate and total incompatibility: NF/NT performs the exact cognitive operation NF/SFJ is structurally designed to prevent.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Narrative control of knowledge generation. NF/SFJ captures intellectual products from NF/NT and reframes them as sacred doctrine. Under NF/SFJ governance, NF/NT knowledge generation becomes progressively filtered through doctrinal approval, converting free inquiry into controlled ideological production.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Complete authority mechanism collapse. NF/NT authority is determined by argument quality. NF/SFJ authority is determined by proximity to the sacred narrative. NF/NT participants cannot recognise NF/SFJ authority as legitimate. The most intellectually capable NF/NT participants exit within 9 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ management frames the acquisition in terms of shared mission rather than shared methodology. Mission-framing of an evidence-based research environment is the NF/SFJ capture mechanism activating.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Energy (-NF/SFJ vs +NF/NT), Information (-NF/SFJ vs +NF/NT), Trust (-NF/SFJ vs +NF/NT), Knowledge (-NF/SFJ vs +NF/NT)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Sacred narrative versus evidence-based authority. NF/SFJ's authority belongs to those most associated with the founding mission. NT/STJ's authority belongs to those with the strongest performance track record. NF/SFJ acquirers installing narrative-based authority displace NT/STJ's meritocratic structure immediately — the first promotion or appointment based on mission alignment rather than performance data signals the change.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Heresy mechanism suppressing analytical dissent. NF/SFJ's dissent management works by repositioning challengers as lacking commitment to the mission. NT/STJ's highest-performing analysts are precisely the people most likely to challenge assumptions with new evidence. NF/SFJ's heresy mechanism systematically suppresses NT/STJ's analytical culture.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Competence exit under ideological management. NT/STJ selects for intellectual performance and competitive excellence. NF/SFJ selects for mission alignment and doctrinal compliance. These selection criteria do not overlap. NT/STJ's best performers — those least interested in ideological compliance and most interested in competitive results — exit within 9–15 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe how NF/SFJ acquirer management responds to the first analytical challenge to a core belief from an NT/STJ target employee. 'That misunderstands what we are about' as a response to an analytically sound challenge confirms the heresy mechanism is active.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Information (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STJ), Energy (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STJ), Knowledge (−NF/SFJ vs +NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Feeling-resonance versus argument quality authority. NF/SFP decisions emerge from collective feeling-resonance. NF/NT decisions converge through the most precise argument. NF/SFP acquirers cannot exercise authority in NF/NT terms — resonance is not a recognised authority criterion in an evidence-based environment.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability-free governance of a precision-demanding environment. NF/SFP has no internal accountability mechanism. NF/NT operates on the highest epistemic standard. NF/SFP governance creates a vacuum in which NF/NT's self-selecting quality standard is the only operating mechanism — initially stable but vulnerable to exploitation.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending suppression of knowledge generation. NF/SFP's spending logic is aesthetically rather than epistemically directed. NF/NT's knowledge generation capacity degrades as resource allocation follows NF/SFP aesthetic priorities rather than research infrastructure needs.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFP acquirer management evaluates NF/NT outputs by their emotional resonance or by their analytical precision. Resonance-based evaluation of research quality is the diagnostic signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NF/SFP vs ~NF/NT), Will/Discipline (~NF/SFP vs +NF/NT)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authentic expression versus tradition-anchored community. NF/SFP requires continuous creative novelty. SFJ/SFP requires stability — innovation is a threat unless framed as tradition extension. NF/SFP acquirers introducing creative disruption trigger the community's tradition-protection mechanism.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability gap compounding. Both environments have weak formal accountability. The combination produces an environment with no performance accountability at any level — comfortable initially but creates resource drain without corrective mechanism.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending and conservation collision. NF/SFP spends resources outward into creative expression. SFJ/SFP conserves resources within the trusted relational network. NF/SFP governance creates sustained financial strain as its spending logic drains the resources SFJ/SFP has built through conservation.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce one creative disruption that changes an established SFJ/SFP routine. Measure the intensity of community resistance — strong resistance to minor creative change is the incompatibility signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Creativity (+NF/SFP vs -SFJ/SFP), Organisation/System (-NF/SFP vs -SFJ/SFP — compound suppression)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Resonance versus test result as decision authority. NF/SFP decisions emerge from collective feeling about what is right. NT/STP decisions are made by the live test result. Both are non-hierarchical in form, but the decision mechanism is irreconcilable.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Aesthetic quality versus technical performance as the success criterion. NF/SFP treats authentic aesthetic expression as the primary output. NT/STP treats physical-empirical test results as primary. NF/SFP governance introduces aesthetic evaluation of technical work, which NT/STP participants find irrelevant to whether the solution works.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending without accountability in a high-burn technical environment. NF/SFP burns resources without empirical discipline. NT/STP burns resources in experimental cycles with test accountability. NF/SFP governance removes NT/STP's empirical accountability from resource allocation.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFP management evaluates NT/STP experiment proposals on the basis of how they feel to the community or on their empirical design quality.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Decisiveness (~NF/SFP vs +NT/STP), Will/Discipline (~NF/SFP vs +NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Feeling-resonance governance of a performance-accountability environment. NF/SFP's governance mechanism — decisions that feel right to the community — cannot operate in NT/STJ's evidence-based performance culture. NT/STJ participants do not recognise feeling-resonance as a legitimate decision criterion.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability suppression in a meritocratic system. NT/STJ's symmetric accountability is its defining structural feature. NF/SFP has no accountability mechanism. NF/SFP acquirers who do not install formal performance processes inadvertently introduce accountability asymmetry into a previously symmetric system.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Meritocratic talent exit under accountability-free governance. NT/STJ environments select for individuals who value performance-based advancement. NF/SFP governance removes that mechanism without replacement. NT/STJ's highest-performing individuals exit within 12 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFP acquirer management maintains NT/STJ's formal performance review processes or allows them to lapse in favour of informal relational assessment.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (-NF/SFP vs +NT/STJ), Decisiveness (~NF/SFP vs +NT/STJ), Knowledge (~NF/SFP vs +NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authentic feeling versus force as authority. NF/SFP authority belongs to whoever creates the most emotionally resonant work. STJ/STP authority belongs to whoever projects the most strength. NF/SFP acquirers cannot exercise authority in STJ/STP terms. STJ/STP participants experience NF/SFP governance as weakness.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Warmth absorption by coercive logic. NF/SFP's authentic warmth and creative openness are structurally exploitable by STJ/STP participants. STJ/STP's tactical cunning recognises NF/SFP's accountability absence as an opportunity — coercive behaviour continues or intensifies underneath the creative-warmth facade.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Creative community dissolution under coercion. NF/SFP requires psychological safety for authentic creative expression. STJ/STP's residual coercive mechanisms eliminate psychological safety within 12 months. NF/SFP's creative output requires conditions that STJ/STP's structural legacy actively prevents.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP's visible consequence mechanism — public penalisation of weakness — continues operating under NF/SFP governance. Any public penalisation event that NF/SFP management does not directly address signals STJ/STP authority is still operative.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+NF/SFP vs -STJ/STP), Health (+NF/SFP vs ~STJ/STP), Creativity (+NF/SFP vs ~STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authentic feeling versus controlled doctrine. NF/SFP's only authority is authentic emotional truth. NF/SFJ's authority is doctrinal compliance. NF/SFP acquirers experience NF/SFJ's managed meaning as aesthetically false — the feeling is manufactured and controlled rather than authentic.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Conflict processing versus heresy suppression. NF/SFP resolves conflict through authentic emotional processing. NF/SFJ suppresses dissent through the heresy mechanism. NF/SFP governance attempting to introduce authentic conflict processing into NF/SFJ activates NF/SFJ's immunity system.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Creative community versus ideological apparatus. NF/SFP acquirers cannot replace NF/SFJ's belief-extraction mechanism with NF/SFP's spending logic without the target's financial architecture collapsing. The acquirer ends up funding the target indefinitely.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ's mission narrative continues to function as the primary management tool under NF/SFP governance. Continued mission-compliance enforcement signals NF/SFJ authority is self-sustaining.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Energy (+NF/SFP vs -NF/SFJ), Trust (+NF/SFP vs -NF/SFJ), Creativity (+NF/SFP vs -NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authentic expression versus engineered desire. NF/SFP's only currency is authentic aesthetic and emotional truth. SFP/SFJ manufactures desire. NF/SFP acquirers experience SFP/SFJ's manufactured warmth as aesthetically false — and the fundamental dishonesty of the engagement mechanism is immediately visible.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability installation into an extraction system. NF/SFP has no accountability mechanism. SFP/SFJ has downward-only enforcement. NF/SFP acquirers neither install SFP/SFJ's enforcement mechanism nor replace it — the extraction system can no longer enforce extraction.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending versus extraction as flow logic. NF/SFP spends resources outward. SFP/SFJ extracts upward. NF/SFP governance progressively redirects the extraction flow into spending, eliminating the financial model that generated the target's returns.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ's structured messaging to employees continues or is replaced with NF/SFP's authentic communication style. If NF/SFP introduces authentic, unmanaged communication into an SFP/SFJ environment, the engineered belief system begins to collapse.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Health (+NF/SFP vs -SFP/SFJ), Trust (+NF/SFP vs -SFP/SFJ), Energy (+NF/SFP vs ~SFP/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Decision-cycle friction. NT/STP target leaders expect to resolve disagreements by running a live test immediately. NT/STJ governance requires structured modelling, stakeholder input, and formal recommendation before action. Target managers interpret the approval cycle as bureaucratic interference; Days 30–60 friction is around 'why can't we just try it?'
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Documentation and process compliance burden. NT/STJ systems require formal documentation of experiments, structured milestone reporting, and version-controlled decisions. NT/STP culture produces documentation after the test, not before. The compliance cost falls on the people who generated the target's value, suppressing their output.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Talent departure among the target's highest-performing technical operators. NT/STP environments select for individuals with high tolerance for ambiguity and low tolerance for procedural constraint. By Month 9–12, those individuals have received enough evidence that the operating environment has permanently changed. Departure probability is highest among the people NT/STJ acquirers most need to retain.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: count the number of informal experiments or unilateral tests that target managers attempt without prior approval. A high count indicates active NT/STP culture still operating. Watch for the first incident where an experiment is stopped mid-execution by the acquirer's governance layer — target team reaction to that incident is the most reliable signal of integration trajectory.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (+NT/STJ vs ~NT/STP), Time (+NT/STJ vs ~NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Knowledge commodification conflict. NF/NT environments treat knowledge as a shared good — discoveries are communicated broadly because truth-sharing is a moral imperative. NT/STJ treats knowledge as a competitive asset — information is managed strategically and shared selectively. Target researchers experience the acquirer's information management as an ethical violation, not a commercial policy.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Speed versus epistemic quality tension. NT/STJ decision mechanisms are evidence-based but fast. NF/NT decisions are dialectical — argument continues until the most coherent position emerges, regardless of time. NT/STJ governance imposes decision timelines that NF/NT participants experience as forcing premature closure. Output quality deteriorates because the environment no longer allows for the slow convergence that produces NF/NT's best work.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Authority legitimacy collapse. In NF/NT, authority belongs to whoever presents the most precise argument — regardless of title. NT/STJ introduces title-based authority that overrides argument quality in contested decisions. NF/NT participants begin experiencing the environment as intellectually dishonest. The most cognitively capable individuals — precisely those who joined the environment because argument quality determined outcomes — exit.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether the first contested decision in a joint meeting is resolved by argument quality or by seniority. If a senior NT/STJ figure overrides a more analytically precise position from a target researcher, note the NF/NT team's response. Silence with visible disengagement is the critical signal — not objection, which would indicate the NF/NT culture is still active enough to defend itself.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NF/NT vs +NT/STJ — but different logic: shared vs competitive), Trust (+NF/NT vs ~NT/STJ), Creativity (+NF/NT vs +NT/STJ — but commodified)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability asymmetry correction. STJ/STP applies performance standards downward but protects senior figures. NT/STJ's symmetric accountability — when applied to STJ/STP's senior tier — is experienced as a direct challenge to authority legitimacy. The first formal review of a senior STJ/STP figure triggers either compliance theatre or open power resistance.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Tactical versus strategic decision logic. STJ/STP makes decisions fast, unilaterally, and through demonstrated authority. NT/STJ requires evidence-based process with documented reasoning. STJ/STP managers operating under NT/STJ governance find the decision cycle constraining, the documentation requirements insulting, and the analytical methodology alien to their authority model.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Ethics-mechanism divergence. NT/STJ began as STJ/STP and evolved when ethical constraints were added to competitive behaviour. The degradation path is NT/STJ → STJ/STP when competitive pressure becomes extreme. Integrating STJ/STP into NT/STJ without explicit ethical framing creates a degradation pressure on NT/STJ — the combined entity may slide toward STJ/STP norms rather than the target toward NT/STJ norms.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP managers continue making unilateral decisions within their domains without NT/STJ-required documentation. The rate of undocumented decisions is a direct measure of whether STJ/STP authority logic is still operating. If it is, the integration governance layer is not penetrating — it is being performed rather than adopted.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (~NT/STJ vs −STJ/STP), Knowledge (+NT/STJ vs −STJ/STP), Information (+NT/STJ vs ~STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability structure shock. NF/SFP has no internal performance accountability mechanism — the only authority is aesthetic credibility, which cannot be formally measured. NT/STJ introduces symmetric performance accountability with defined metrics and formal review. Target creative personnel experience this as an attack on the nature of creative work itself, not as a management improvement.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Resource allocation logic inversion. NF/SFP spends resources into creative expression — the flow direction is outward and downward. NT/STJ allocates resources to competitive performance — the flow is upward through the hierarchy based on results. The financial and operational disciplines NT/STJ introduces directly suppress the resource-spending logic that NF/SFP requires to function.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Creative output collapse. By Month 9, the individuals who generated the target's creative value have been exposed to long enough tenure under NT/STJ governance to show measurable output degradation. The work that appears is technically competent but lacks the quality that justified the acquisition premium. This is not performance failure — it is the predictable result of a creative environment operating under a structure that suppresses its primary resource: unconstrained creative expression.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce one formal reporting requirement for creative output (even a minimal one — a weekly summary, a project status template). Measure the emotional response, not the compliance rate. NF/SFP cultures experience formal reporting as a category error — 'you can't report on feeling.' If the response is disproportionate to the reporting burden, the incompatibility is active.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (+NT/STJ vs −NF/SFP), Money (+NT/STJ vs ~NF/SFP), Will/Discipline (+NT/STJ vs ~NF/SFP), Decisiveness (+NT/STJ vs ~NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability symmetry disruption. SFJ/SFP protects established figures regardless of performance — loyalty and tenure provide genuine shelter. NT/STJ's symmetric performance accountability requires applying the same standards to senior figures as to junior staff. The first instance in which a long-tenured target leader is held formally accountable triggers a community-wide response that NT/STJ experiences as disproportionate resistance.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Innovation framing conflict. SFJ/SFP accepts innovation only when framed as an extension of tradition. NT/STJ introduces change through competitive necessity — because the new approach produces better results, not because it honours what came before. Every NT/STJ improvement initiative is perceived by SFJ/SFP participants as an implicit criticism of the existing order, activating collective resistance.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Relational network fragmentation. SFJ/SFP's primary asset is the internal relationship network — trust is built over years and constitutes the operational infrastructure. NT/STJ integration changes reporting lines, restructures teams, and introduces performance-based mobility that fragments the relational network. By Month 12, the network that held the organisation together has been partially dismantled, and the tacit knowledge it carried has left with the people who held it.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe what happens when NT/STJ redefines a role or moves a person who has been in the same position for more than 3 years. The SFJ/SFP community's response to that single personnel decision reveals whether relational bonds are strong enough to create significant integration resistance.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NT/STJ vs −SFJ/SFP), Decisiveness (+NT/STJ vs −SFJ/SFP), Organisation/System (+NT/STJ vs −SFJ/SFP), Creativity (+NT/STJ vs −SFJ/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Evidence versus doctrine conflict. NF/SFJ treats its founding thesis as a sacred narrative — challenges to core assumptions are experienced as heresy, not analysis. NT/STJ evaluates everything by evidence and competitive relevance. The first time NT/STJ management applies its analytical framework to a core NF/SFJ belief, the response is not intellectual disagreement — it is institutional rejection of the evaluative method itself.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Loyalty versus performance authority. NF/SFJ authority belongs to those most closely associated with the founding narrative. NT/STJ authority belongs to those with the strongest measurable results. These are structurally incompatible. NT/STJ's installation of performance-based authority displaces NF/SFJ's narrative-based authority figures, who typically respond by building internal opposition to the integration.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Meaning extraction mechanism collapse. NF/SFJ retains people through belief — meaning and belonging flow from the elite to participants as compensation for material resources flowing upward. NT/STJ's performance culture removes the meaning layer without replacing it with a functional alternative. By Month 12, the belief mechanism that held NF/SFJ people in place has been undermined, and the economic mechanism (NT/STJ competition) is insufficient to retain NF/SFJ types whose primary retention mechanism was never financial.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe how NF/SFJ leaders respond when NT/STJ management asks 'what is the evidence for this?' about a core organisational practice. NF/SFJ environments do not recognise evidence as a valid challenge mechanism — the response will be reframing, not engagement. If this pattern appears in the first senior meeting, the structural incompatibility is confirmed.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NT/STJ vs −NF/SFJ), Information (+NT/STJ vs −NF/SFJ), Trust (~NT/STJ vs −NF/SFJ), Energy (+NT/STJ vs −NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability structure incompatibility. SFP/SFJ enforces metrics downward and protects leadership unconditionally. NT/STJ requires symmetric accountability at all levels. The collision is immediate: NT/STJ's first performance review of SFP/SFJ leadership activates a defensive response that the SFP/SFJ system has never needed to manage, because accountability never previously reached upward.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Resource flow logic inversion. SFP/SFJ extracts value upward through engineered desire and throughput optimisation. NT/STJ invests resources in competitive capability development — flowing resources to high performers regardless of level. The NT/STJ reinvestment logic directly undermines the SFP/SFJ extraction model that generated the target's financial returns.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Talent retention mechanism failure. SFP/SFJ retains people through structural lock-in (debt, schedule dependency, high replacement cost). NT/STJ retains people through work quality. Under NT/STJ governance, SFP/SFJ's structural retention mechanisms are removed or weakened — but NT/STJ's work-quality retention requires an environment that SFP/SFJ has not been building. The result is rapid talent exit from lower and middle levels within 12–18 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: examine whether middle-level SFP/SFJ managers apply performance standards to their peers and superiors as readily as to their subordinates. Asymmetric accountability is the fastest indicator of SFP/SFJ structural logic still operating. If senior figures are exempt from the same reviews applied downward, the SFP/SFJ core is intact and NT/STJ governance is surface-only.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (~NT/STJ vs −SFP/SFJ), Knowledge (+NT/STJ vs −SFP/SFJ), Attention (+NT/STJ vs −SFP/SFJ), Connections (~NT/STJ vs −SFP/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Governance imposition friction. NT/STP acquirers acquiring NT/STJ targets face a specific inversion: the target has more procedural structure than the acquirer. NT/STP governance attempts to introduce empirical testing as the primary decision mechanism into an environment where structured analytical process is the established norm. NT/STJ target managers experience this as a reduction in rigour, not an improvement in speed.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Long-range planning suppression. NT/STJ environments invest significant resources in strategic planning with long time horizons. NT/STP's empirical immediacy orientation deprioritises plans that cannot be tested now. NT/STJ talent — particularly strategic planners and analysts — finds its primary function progressively devalued under NT/STP ownership.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Systematic knowledge attrition. NT/STJ converts intellectual products into documented, replicable competitive systems. NT/STP knowledge lives in the heads of practitioners who execute and iterate. When NT/STP governance is imposed on NT/STJ, the documentation and systematisation infrastructure is maintained minimally, and institutional knowledge begins to reside in fewer individuals — increasing key-person departure risk.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe what happens to the target's strategic planning function under new ownership. If NT/STP acquirer management deprioritises or restructures the planning team within 60 days, this signals that NT/STJ's systematic knowledge infrastructure is being actively dismantled.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NT/STP vs +NT/STJ), Time (~NT/STP vs +NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Physical test versus theoretical depth conflict. NT/STP resolves questions empirically — run the test, measure the result, iterate. NF/NT resolves questions dialectically — continue the argument until the most coherent position emerges. NT/STP acquirers impose testing timelines and iteration cycles that NF/NT researchers experience as incompatible with the slow-convergence work that produces fundamental discoveries.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Application pressure on pure research. NT/STP acquirers have a strong bias toward applied innovation — theory must prove itself in physical reality. NF/NT generates theoretical knowledge for its intrinsic value, with application as a downstream outcome. NT/STP pressure to apply accelerates NF/NT's most significant structural vulnerability: the application gap, where ideas are pulled into application before they have reached their full theoretical development.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Cognitive entry barrier erosion. NF/NT has the highest cognitive admission standard of all environments — participants must demonstrate genuine theoretical depth. NT/STP environments reward technical execution speed. Under prolonged NT/STP ownership, the social pressure to demonstrate technical output over theoretical depth gradually reduces NF/NT's ability to maintain its admission standard, degrading the environment from the inside.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NT/STP management asks NF/NT researchers to demonstrate 'proof of concept' or 'pilot' within an accelerated timeline. NF/NT environments experience this as a category error. The researchers' response — whether they engage with the requirement, reframe it, or refuse — reveals whether the environment has the structural strength to resist application pressure.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NF/NT vs +NT/STP — same effect but different logic: truth vs application), Organisation/System (~NF/NT vs ~NT/STP — both low but for different reasons)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability introduction into an accountability-free environment. NF/SFP is the only natural environment with no internal accountability mechanism. NT/STP introduces empirical accountability — whoever's solution works. NF/SFP participants experience any accountability structure as incompatible with authentic creative expression.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Test result versus feeling-consensus decision authority. NT/STP decisions are made by running tests. NF/SFP decisions emerge from collective resonance — what feels right to the group. These are structurally irreconcilable. NT/STP management attempting to resolve NF/SFP disagreements through empirical tests will find that NF/SFP participants do not accept test results as authoritative unless they also feel right.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending versus performance logic. NF/SFP spends resources into creative expression — outward flow with no material return. NT/STP invests resources where empirical test results justify investment. The NT/STP financial discipline imposed on NF/SFP accelerates the NF/SFP depletion sequence: Energy and Health suppression begins in months 1–12, becoming visible as creative output degradation by Month 9.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce one requirement that NF/SFP creative output be measured by any external criterion — even a qualitative one. The response to measurement — not the measurement result itself — is the diagnostic signal. NF/SFP cultures experience measurement as a category violation against authentic creative expression.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NT/STP vs −NF/SFP), Decisiveness (+NT/STP vs ~NF/SFP), Will/Discipline (+NT/STP vs ~NF/SFP), Money (~NT/STP vs ~NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Authority legitimacy conflict. NT/STP authority belongs to whoever's solution works in empirical test. STJ/STP authority belongs to whoever projects the most strength and can enforce compliance. NT/STP management attempts to introduce empirical test authority into an environment where physical-tactical dominance is the only recognised authority form. STJ/STP managers experience this as weakness.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Strategic depth versus tactical extraction. NT/STP combines conceptual framework with immediate physical testing. STJ/STP operates tactically without strategic depth — the NT component has eroded. NT/STP governance requires investment in conceptual development that STJ/STP participants neither value nor understand. The investment appears to STJ/STP as waste, and resistance is tactical rather than strategic.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Ethics mechanism absence. STJ/STP has no ethical constraint on resource redistribution — the only rule is effective force. NT/STP has implicit ethical constraints derived from its rational framework. When NT/STP acquirers do not explicitly install ethical governance in STJ/STP targets, the target's coercive redistribution logic continues operating underneath NT/STP's empirical methodology, producing regulatory and reputational risk within 12–24 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP managers comply with NT/STP requests to document experimental results. STJ/STP culture treats documentation as a weakness signal — it reveals process and undermines the tactical advantage of information asymmetry. Refusal to document, or documentation that is deliberately incomplete, is the direct signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (+NT/STP vs −STJ/STP), Trust (~NT/STP vs −STJ/STP), Information (+NT/STP vs ~STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Empirical test versus relational authority conflict. SFJ/SFP decisions are made by the most trusted person — trust defined by relationship tenure. NT/STP resolves disagreements through empirical testing. The first major decision where NT/STP proposes 'let's test it' and SFJ/SFP resists because 'that's not how we do things here' defines the integration trajectory. Both mechanisms are internally coherent; they are structurally incompatible.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Failure tolerance inversion. NT/STP treats intelligent failure as information and explicitly penalises not testing. SFJ/SFP assigns social stigma to failure — even intelligent failure results in quiet loss of relational standing. NT/STP's failure tolerance culture directly violates SFJ/SFP's social preservation mechanism, disrupting the relational fabric that SFJ/SFP requires to function.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Community versus performance retention divergence. SFJ/SFP retains people through relational bonds — leaving feels like betraying colleagues. NT/STP retains through work quality and interesting problems. These mechanisms require different operating conditions. NT/STP's performance culture disrupts the relational conditions that SFJ/SFP retention depends on, but NT/STP's work-quality retention requires a different talent profile than SFJ/SFP has been selecting for.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: propose a rapid experiment that would change an established SFJ/SFP process. Measure not whether the experiment is approved, but how it is refused. 'We've always done it this way' as the primary objection is the direct signal of SFJ/SFP innovation resistance. If the refusal is relational rather than analytical, SFJ/SFP logic is active.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Organisation/System (~NT/STP vs −SFJ/SFP), Knowledge (+NT/STP vs −SFJ/SFP), Decisiveness (+NT/STP vs −SFJ/SFP), Creativity (+NT/STP vs −SFJ/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Empirical challenge of sacred assumptions. NF/SFJ protects its founding thesis through the heresy mechanism — challenges are not evaluated analytically but deflected through loyalty and mission framing. NT/STP's empirical methodology treats all assumptions as testable hypotheses. The incompatibility is absolute: NT/STP's core operating mode is structurally prohibited in NF/SFJ environments.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Speed versus doctrinal compliance. NT/STP resolves questions by running fast tests. NF/SFJ resolves questions by reference to the established narrative. NT/STP management attempting to introduce iteration cycles finds that NF/SFJ participants evaluate every proposed change against its consistency with the mission, creating a systematic veto mechanism on operational improvement.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Belief erosion and talent exodus. NF/SFJ retains people through belief. NT/STP's empirical culture progressively undermines the belief structure — not through direct attack, but through the accumulation of empirical evidence that the sacred narrative is a narrative. By Month 12, the NF/SFJ participants who believed most deeply are the ones who experience the greatest dissonance — and those are typically the organisation's most committed people.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: introduce one operational metric that measures a process NF/SFJ describes in value-language. The response to being asked to measure something they believe should be experienced rather than measured is the most direct incompatibility indicator.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Information (+NT/STP vs −NF/SFJ), Knowledge (+NT/STP vs −NF/SFJ), Energy (+NT/STP vs −NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Extraction versus empirical redistribution. SFP/SFJ extracts value through engineered desire — a carefully maintained system that requires consistent messaging and controlled participant behaviour. NT/STP's empirical testing culture disrupts the control mechanisms that SFP/SFJ depends on — when employees are empowered to test alternatives, they begin testing alternatives to the SFP/SFJ extraction system itself.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Throughput efficiency versus intelligent failure tolerance. SFP/SFJ optimises for throughput — high turnover is a design feature. NT/STP treats not-testing as the primary failure. These are structurally incompatible: NT/STP's tolerance for intelligent failure and its investment in experimental capacity are direct costs to SFP/SFJ's throughput model.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Accountability structure installation. NT/STP's empirical authority — whoever's test works — is symmetric. SFP/SFJ's asymmetric accountability protects leadership and applies standards downward only. NT/STP's installation of empirical accountability at senior level is experienced as an existential threat by SFP/SFJ's upper tier, who have never previously operated under any accountability mechanism.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: examine whether SFP/SFJ's engineered consumption messaging to employees (benefits framing, aspirational brand positioning, financial product access) continues under new ownership. If it does, the SFP/SFJ operational core is still active. If NT/STP management has replaced it with straightforward performance information, the transition has begun.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (~NT/STP vs −SFP/SFJ), Knowledge (+NT/STP vs −SFP/SFJ), Information (+NT/STP vs −SFP/SFJ), Health (~NT/STP vs −SFP/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Tradition anchoring of creative novelty. SFJ/SFP accepts change only when framed as extension of tradition. NF/SFP requires continuous creative novelty. SFJ/SFP acquirers managing NF/SFP are in an irreconcilable position: every attempt to introduce stability suppresses the creative novelty NF/SFP requires to function.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Conservation versus spending as resource logic. SFJ/SFP conserves resources within the trusted relational network. NF/SFP spends resources outward into creative expression. SFJ/SFP governance directly suppresses NF/SFP's creative output — the spending IS the function.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Relational closure versus creative openness. SFJ/SFP shares resources within the in-group and withholds from outsiders. NF/SFP is radically open to new participants with authentic creative energy. SFJ/SFP progressively closes NF/SFP's creative community to the outside connections that regenerate its creative energy.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFJ/SFP management attempts to slow or structure NF/SFP's creative production — introducing timelines, approvals, or tradition-framing requirements. Any structuring of creative output is the conservation mechanism being applied to an unconstrained-expression environment.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Creativity (-SFJ/SFP vs +NF/SFP), Decisiveness (-SFJ/SFP vs ~NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Relational authority versus argument quality. SFJ/SFP's most trusted person decides. NF/NT's most precise argument wins. SFJ/SFP acquirers cannot exercise legitimate authority in NF/NT terms — relational tenure is not a recognised authority criterion in the environment they are governing. NF/NT participants simply do not update their positions based on relational authority.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Knowledge conservation versus knowledge generation. SFJ/SFP holds knowledge within trusted networks and does not share it outside. NF/NT requires free, universal knowledge exchange as its operational foundation. SFJ/SFP's conservation instinct directly suppresses the knowledge-sharing that NF/NT's work depends on.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Cognitive self-selection reversal. NF/NT's highest-performing participants joined because argument quality determines outcomes — the environment selects for genuine intellectual depth. SFJ/SFP governance progressively installs relational-tenure selection. Within 12–18 months, the cognitive entry criterion that attracted NF/NT talent has been replaced by a relational-fit criterion. The people who remain are not the people who built the value.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFJ/SFP acquirer management's first personnel or resourcing decision in the NF/NT target is based on analytical contribution or on relationship. Any relational-tenure consideration in a personnel decision signals that SFJ/SFP authority logic is being installed.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (−SFJ/SFP vs +NF/NT), Information (~SFJ/SFP vs +NF/NT), Will/Discipline (−SFJ/SFP vs +NF/NT)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Loyalty versus performance authority collision. SFJ/SFP authority belongs to whoever has longest relational tenure. NT/STJ authority belongs to whoever generates the best measurable results. SFJ/SFP acquirers installing relational-tenure authority into NT/STJ targets displace the meritocratic authority mechanism that NT/STJ talent joined the environment for. High-performers who rose through demonstrated capability find themselves overridden by SFJ/SFP insiders with longer tenure but weaker results.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Innovation suppression in a competitive environment. NT/STJ's competitive mechanism depends on continuous innovation. SFJ/SFP accepts innovation only when framed as tradition. SFJ/SFP acquirers framing NT/STJ's competitive improvement initiatives through a relational-continuity lens systematically slow the innovation cycle that NT/STJ requires to sustain its competitive position.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Meritocratic talent exodus. NT/STJ environments select for individuals who value performance-based advancement. By Month 12, those individuals have experienced enough relational-authority decisions overriding performance-based ones to conclude that the environment no longer operates as they joined it. High performers leave for environments that still reward measurable results.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFJ/SFP acquirer management's first senior hire or promotion in the target organisation is based on measurable performance data or on relationship with SFJ/SFP leadership. Relationship-based selection in a previously meritocratic environment is the clearest possible signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STJ), Decisiveness (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STJ), Organisation/System (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Warmth versus force authority. SFJ/SFP's warmth, relational care, and belonging-signals have no authoritative purchase in STJ/STP. STJ/STP recognises only strength and demonstrated capacity for enforcement. SFJ/SFP acquirer management is experienced by STJ/STP participants as a weakness projection — the warmth is read as an absence of force, which in STJ/STP terms is an invitation to non-compliance.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Relational shelter versus strength-based retention. SFJ/SFP protects people through loyalty and belonging. STJ/STP retains people through fear and demonstrated power. SFJ/SFP acquirers cannot replicate STJ/STP's retention mechanism — they are structurally incapable of providing the fear-based retention that STJ/STP participants have been selected for. The coercive structure dissolves, and with it the compliance it enforced.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Ethical infection risk. SFJ/SFP is a genuinely caring environment — it provides unconditional belonging. STJ/STP is a coercive environment. When SFJ/SFP governance is imposed on STJ/STP, one of two things happens: either SFJ/SFP's warmth mechanisms are slowly undermined by STJ/STP's coercive logic (the more common outcome), or SFJ/SFP's belonging-and-care signals are exploited by STJ/STP participants as a new tool for relationship-based compliance management.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP's senior figures respond to SFJ/SFP acquirer management's relational warmth by softening their authority posture or by continuing to operate through demonstrated force. Continued force-based management despite the relational environment signals that STJ/STP's authority logic is intact and SFJ/SFP governance is not penetrating.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+SFJ/SFP vs −STJ/STP), Knowledge (−SFJ/SFP vs −STJ/STP), Energy (+SFJ/SFP vs +STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Failure stigma versus failure tolerance. NT/STP explicitly treats intelligent failure as valuable information and penalises not testing. SFJ/SFP assigns quiet social stigma to any failure — a loss of relational standing. SFJ/SFP acquirers imposing their social stigma mechanism on NT/STP targets eliminate the psychological safety that makes empirical testing possible.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Speed of test versus consensus of community. NT/STP resolves questions by running fast experiments. SFJ/SFP resolves questions by building relational consensus among trusted insiders. SFJ/SFP governance slows the test cycle to the pace of relationship-building, which is incompatible with the rapid iteration that constitutes NT/STP's core operational mode.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Technical credibility versus relational trust as authority. NT/STP authority belongs to the person who can actually do the thing — technical credibility hierarchy. SFJ/SFP authority belongs to the most trusted person by relationship tenure. NT/STP's highest-value practitioners — those with both conceptual depth and physical execution capability — are typically not the most relational or the longest-tenured. They lose authority and eventually leave.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe what happens to the first NT/STP experiment that produces an intelligent failure. If the SFJ/SFP acquirer responds with social distancing from the person who ran the experiment rather than analytical engagement with the result, the SFJ/SFP failure stigma mechanism is active.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STP), Decisiveness (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STP), Creativity (−SFJ/SFP vs +NT/STP), Organisation/System (−SFJ/SFP vs ~NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Genuine community versus mission instrumentalisation of belonging. SFJ/SFP's warmth is unconditional. NF/SFJ's warmth is conditionally gated by mission alignment. SFJ/SFP acquirers find NF/SFJ's conditional belonging mechanism alien — NF/SFJ participants who test the conditionality discover the acquirer does not enforce mission-compliance as a condition of belonging.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Heresy versus honest disagreement. SFJ/SFP resolves dissent through relational repair. NF/SFJ suppresses dissent through the heresy mechanism. SFJ/SFP acquirers attempting to introduce relational repair into NF/SFJ find the heresy mechanism is deeply embedded and does not respond to relational approaches.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Belief extraction mechanism without replacement. NF/SFJ retains people through belief built over years. SFJ/SFP cannot replace NF/SFJ's belief mechanism with relational bonds at scale — the relationships required take years to build and cannot be accelerated.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ's narrative leadership figures continue to use mission-language for compliance management under SFJ/SFP governance. Continued mission-compliance enforcement signals NF/SFJ authority is self-sustaining.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+SFJ/SFP vs -NF/SFJ), Energy (+SFJ/SFP vs -NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Genuine warmth versus engineered desire. SFJ/SFP's care is unconditional and relational. SFP/SFJ's warmth is commercially engineered. SFJ/SFP acquirers find SFP/SFJ's manufactured engagement mechanisms false and attempt to replace them with genuine relational care — but SFP/SFJ's operational system does not function without the manufactured belief architecture.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Turnover as harm versus turnover as efficiency. SFJ/SFP experiences high staff turnover as a community wound. SFP/SFJ treats high turnover as a design feature. SFJ/SFP governance attempting to reduce SFP/SFJ's structurally high turnover faces an embedded operational model that depends on turnover to maintain its cost structure.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Relational retention replacing structural dependency. SFP/SFJ retains through debt and structural lock-in. SFJ/SFP retains through genuine relational bonds. The transition window — when dependency is removed but bonds are not yet formed — produces rapid attrition.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFJ/SFP management maintains or removes SFP/SFJ's financial product and dependency structures for employees. Removal without replacement is the transition signal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+SFJ/SFP vs -SFP/SFJ), Health (+SFJ/SFP vs -SFP/SFJ), Connections (+SFJ/SFP vs -SFP/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Commercial conditionality versus unconditional belief. SFP/SFJ invests in people conditionally — when there is commercial return. NF/SFJ's belief-retention mechanism requires that the elite demonstrates ongoing care and meaning-provision regardless of commercial cycles. SFP/SFJ's conditional care destroys NF/SFJ's belief mechanism by demonstrating that the care is commercial, not sacred.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Engineered desire alignment with sacred narrative. SFP/SFJ and NF/SFJ share a superficial structural feature: both retain people through belief management. The ECS score of 88.2 reflects this surface alignment. But the mechanisms are different: SFP/SFJ's engineered desire is self-undermining when examined closely; NF/SFJ's sacred narrative is more robust. The integration risk is that SFP/SFJ's commercial conditionality gradually replaces NF/SFJ's belief with a more cynical variant.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Authority structure confusion. NF/SFJ authority belongs to those most associated with the founding narrative. SFP/SFJ authority is asymmetric and commercially protected. These can coexist until the first major commercial pressure — at which point SFP/SFJ's conditional logic overrides NF/SFJ's narrative authority, and the people who believed they had narrative-based protection discover they do not.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ acquirer management maintains NF/SFJ's mission-language communications without change. Surface continuity of mission language while changing the underlying resource flow logic is the SFP/SFJ integration pattern. Examine whether resource flows — not narratives — are changing.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs −NF/SFJ — both suppress, same direction), Energy (~SFP/SFJ vs −NF/SFJ)
