frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Engineered compliance versus force-based compliance. SFP/SFJ obtains compliance through manufactured desire and structural dependency. STJ/STP obtains compliance through demonstrated force and fear. These are different mechanisms that select for different participant profiles. SFP/SFJ's dependency-engineering fails on STJ/STP participants who operate through force-recognition rather than desire-recognition.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Throughput versus strength-based elimination. SFP/SFJ handles underperformance through turnover — replace quickly and cheaply. STJ/STP handles it through public humiliation and force-based displacement. SFP/SFJ's throughput governance lacks the force mechanism STJ/STP requires to maintain its internal hierarchy. Without the force mechanism, STJ/STP's strength-based authority structure loses its enforcement tool.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Extraction mechanism competition. Both SFP/SFJ and STJ/STP are extraction environments — both move resources upward. But their extraction mechanisms differ, and the participant profiles they attract differ. When SFP/SFJ governance is imposed on STJ/STP participants, neither extraction mechanism works optimally — the SFP/SFJ desire engineering does not hold force-selected participants, and STJ/STP's enforcement cannot be performed by SFP/SFJ management.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP's internal compliance mechanism — the visible consequence for non-compliance — continues operating under SFP/SFJ governance. If STJ/STP managers are still publicly penalising weakness without SFP/SFJ management intervention, the STJ/STP authority structure is self-sustaining and SFP/SFJ governance is nominal.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs −STJ/STP — both suppress), Reputation (−SFP/SFJ vs +STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Engineered warmth versus genuine community. SFP/SFJ performs warmth commercially — the facade is warm because warm is profitable. SFJ/SFP warmth is structurally genuine — it is unconditional and relational. SFP/SFJ acquirers initially appear compatible because the warmth signal is similar. The divergence emerges when SFP/SFJ's conditional logic first overrides SFJ/SFP's unconditional care.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Throughput versus community stability. SFP/SFJ treats high turnover as efficient. SFJ/SFP treats departure as a community loss and genuine harm. SFP/SFJ's throughput governance — applied to SFJ/SFP's community — introduces a staff turnover level that SFJ/SFP experiences as catastrophic even when it is within SFP/SFJ's normal operating range. The community's relational infrastructure fragments faster than SFP/SFJ's throughput model recognises as a problem.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Debt-based retention in a loyalty-based community. SFP/SFJ installs structural dependency — financial products, schedule constraints, high replacement costs — as the primary retention mechanism. SFJ/SFP's relational retention does not require or want structural dependency. SFJ/SFP people who recognise that they are being retained through manufactured dependency rather than genuine belonging experience this as the community's fundamental betrayal.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ acquirer management maintains unconditional investment in SFJ/SFP employee wellbeing when commercial pressure arises. The first cost-reduction decision that targets benefits or relational investment is the moment the SFP/SFJ conditional logic becomes visible to SFJ/SFP participants.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP), Connections (−SFP/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP), Health (−SFP/SFJ vs +SFJ/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Commercial conditioning of authentic creative expression. SFP/SFJ manufactures desire for its products. NF/SFP creates authentic aesthetic experience. SFP/SFJ acquirers attempt to apply commercial conditioning to NF/SFP creative output — directing creative energy toward commercially optimised products. NF/SFP participants experience this as the destruction of the only currency their environment recognises: authentic creative truth.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Accountability installation into an accountability-free environment. NF/SFP has no internal accountability mechanism. SFP/SFJ has asymmetric accountability — downward enforcement, upward exemption. SFP/SFJ's downward enforcement mechanism is experienced by NF/SFP as a novel and alien authority structure for which the environment has no established resistance or adaptation mechanism.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Resource spending suppression. NF/SFP spends resources into creative expression unconditionally — this is the environment's fundamental operating logic. SFP/SFJ extracts resources upward through engineered desire. These flow directions are opposite. SFP/SFJ governance progressively redirects NF/SFP's outward resource spending into upward resource extraction, eliminating the environmental condition that produced NF/SFP's creative output.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ acquirer management attempts to monetise NF/SFP's creative output through commercial channels that NF/SFP did not previously use. Forced commercial application of creative work is the SFP/SFJ extraction mechanism operating on NF/SFP's primary resource.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs +NF/SFP), Health (−SFP/SFJ vs +NF/SFP), Energy (~SFP/SFJ vs +NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Throughput optimisation versus experimental investment. SFP/SFJ optimises for high throughput and low cost per unit. NT/STP requires significant investment in experimental capacity. SFP/SFJ's cost discipline eliminates the experimental budget that NT/STP's innovation depends on.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Asymmetric accountability versus empirical test authority. SFP/SFJ protects leadership from accountability. NT/STP grants authority to whoever's test produces the best result. SFP/SFJ's senior protection installed in NT/STP means test results unfavourable to senior positions are overridden, destroying the empirical authority mechanism.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Engineered desire applied to technical talent. SFP/SFJ retains people through manufactured belonging and structural dependency. NT/STP's technical talent joined because the work itself is the retention mechanism. SFP/SFJ's engineered retention is experienced by NT/STP practitioners as transparent manipulation — accelerating departure among the most technically capable.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: examine whether SFP/SFJ management reduces NT/STP's experimental budget in any cost optimisation exercise. Any reduction in resources allocated to experiments signals SFP/SFJ's throughput logic being applied to NT/STP's resource model.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (-SFP/SFJ vs +NT/STP), Trust (-SFP/SFJ vs ~NT/STP), Decisiveness (-SFP/SFJ vs +NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Engineered belief versus evidence-based knowledge. SFP/SFJ's operating mechanism depends on participants believing things that are constructed rather than evidentially true. NF/NT's operating mechanism depends on the progressive revision of beliefs based on evidence. These are structurally irreconcilable: NF/NT's core function — rigorous inquiry — systematically dismantles the belief architecture SFP/SFJ requires.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Knowledge suppression in a knowledge-generation environment. SFP/SFJ suppresses knowledge about the extraction mechanism as an operational necessity. NF/NT generates and shares knowledge as a moral imperative. SFP/SFJ governance of NF/NT faces an irreducible conflict: every piece of knowledge NF/NT generates and shares potentially reveals the SFP/SFJ extraction system to participants.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Cognitive talent exit at maximum speed. NF/NT has the highest cognitive entry standard of all environments. SFP/SFJ is optimised for low-cognitive-investment throughput. The talent that constitutes NF/NT's value — people who joined because truth is the only currency — identifies the SFP/SFJ extraction mechanism faster than any other talent pool. They exit first, they exit quickly, and they are the most difficult to replace.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFP/SFJ acquirer management introduces any information management or messaging controls over NF/NT researchers' communications — with each other, with other institutions, or with the public. Any filtering or control of knowledge output signals the SFP/SFJ suppression mechanism being installed.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs +NF/NT), Knowledge (−SFP/SFJ vs +NF/NT), Energy (~SFP/SFJ vs +NF/NT)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Engineered desire versus performance transparency. SFP/SFJ operates through constructed belief — participants are managed through engineered information about their prospects and value. NT/STJ's symmetric accountability and performance transparency directly undermine the belief management that SFP/SFJ requires. NT/STJ target managers who understand performance data now understand the extraction mechanism operating above them.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Throughput model versus capability investment. SFP/SFJ treats high turnover as efficient — new employees cost less and work harder. NT/STJ invests in capability development — the environment deliberately builds people through competitive pressure. SFP/SFJ governance of NT/STJ eliminates the investment logic that NT/STJ talent joined for, replacing it with throughput optimisation that NT/STJ high-performers recognise immediately and exit.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Meritocratic authority displacement. NT/STJ authority belongs to those with the best measurable results. SFP/SFJ authority is asymmetric — leadership is structurally exempt from the standards applied downward. By Month 9, NT/STJ's performance-based authority structure has been replaced by SFP/SFJ's asymmetric protection of leadership, and the meritocratic talent that built the target's value has identified the pattern and is planning exit.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: examine whether SFP/SFJ acquirer management introduces any financial product, benefits scheme, or compensation structure that creates structural dependency for NT/STJ employees. Debt-based retention, BNPL benefits programmes, or complex vesting schemes applied to a previously merit-retained workforce is the SFP/SFJ extraction mechanism being installed.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (−SFP/SFJ vs +NT/STJ), Trust (−SFP/SFJ vs ~NT/STJ), Information (−SFP/SFJ vs +NT/STJ), Attention (−SFP/SFJ vs +NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Accountability asymmetry reimposition. NT/STJ's symmetric accountability is its primary differentiator from STJ/STP. STJ/STP acquirers installing asymmetric accountability — standards downward but exemption upward — immediately undermine the meritocratic authority that NT/STJ talent joined the environment for. The signal is received within 30 days: if senior figures are exempt from the performance standards applied to subordinates, NT/STJ culture is being replaced.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Coercive redistribution versus competitive performance. STJ/STP takes resources from those without the power to defend them and transfers them upward. NT/STJ distributes resources based on performance outcomes. STJ/STP governance of an NT/STJ organisation redirects resource flows upward through the hierarchy — the NT/STJ performance incentive structure degrades as the coercive extraction structure is installed.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Intellectual versus physical authority. NT/STJ's authority is earned through intellectual performance and measurable results. STJ/STP's authority is held through demonstrated physical or social dominance. NT/STJ's highest-performing intellectual contributors are not selected by STJ/STP's authority logic — they are typically the people least able to project dominance. They exit within 12 months.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP acquirer leadership applies the same review and consequence process to a senior NT/STJ target figure as they would to a junior one. Asymmetric treatment of the first significant performance issue at senior level is the clearest and fastest confirmation that STJ/STP accountability logic is replacing NT/STJ's symmetric structure.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−STJ/STP vs ~NT/STJ), Knowledge (−STJ/STP vs +NT/STJ), Connections (~STJ/STP vs ~NT/STJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Empirical authority versus force authority. NT/STP grants authority to whoever's solution works in a live test. STJ/STP grants authority through demonstrated force. STJ/STP acquirers cannot operate inside NT/STP's empirical test mechanism — the test result does not carry authority in STJ/STP terms unless it also demonstrates power. NT/STP participants who win empirical tests but cannot project dominance find their results ignored.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Failure tolerance elimination. NT/STP's highest value is not-testing as failure. STJ/STP makes no distinction between intelligent and unintelligent failure — failure is weakness, and weakness is the primary selection criterion for elimination. STJ/STP governance eliminates NT/STP's failure-tolerance infrastructure within months, making experimentation too personally costly to sustain.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Technical expertise devaluation. NT/STP's authority hierarchy is built on demonstrated technical and physical competence — the person who can actually do the thing. STJ/STP's hierarchy is built on demonstrated dominance. These are different selection criteria that produce different people at the top. STJ/STP acquirers systematically displace NT/STP's technical leadership with dominance-based leadership, producing organisations that maintain the appearance of technical competence while losing its substance.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe how STJ/STP management responds to the first empirical test result that contradicts a senior figure's position. If the test result is overridden by authority assertion rather than counter-evidence, the empirical test mechanism — NT/STP's core decision authority — has been eliminated.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Knowledge (−STJ/STP vs +NT/STP), Trust (−STJ/STP vs ~NT/STP), Creativity (~STJ/STP vs +NT/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Force versus argument quality. The authority mechanism mismatch here is complete and immediate. NF/NT authority belongs to the most precise argument. STJ/STP authority belongs to whoever can enforce compliance. STJ/STP management exercising force-based authority in an NF/NT environment is immediately recognised by NF/NT participants as intellectually illegitimate — and in NF/NT terms, illegitimate authority is not authority at all.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Knowledge commodification and extraction. NF/NT generates and shares knowledge as a moral imperative. STJ/STP extracts resources from those who cannot defend them — and in NF/NT environments, knowledge is the primary resource. STJ/STP's coercive logic applied to NF/NT converts knowledge from a freely generated and shared good into a controlled, extracted commodity. The generation mechanism collapses.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Cognitive talent elimination. NF/NT has the highest cognitive entry barrier of all environments. STJ/STP selects for physical and tactical dominance. These selection criteria do not overlap. Within 12 months, STJ/STP's authority installation has removed or sidelined the cognitively capable individuals who constituted the NF/NT environment. The organisation retains the form of an NF/NT environment while operating through STJ/STP logic.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP management's first personnel decision in the NF/NT target is based on intellectual contribution or on demonstrated compliance with STJ/STP authority. Any force-based or compliance-based personnel decision signals that STJ/STP logic is being installed in an environment that cannot survive it.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (−STJ/STP vs +NF/NT), Knowledge (−STJ/STP vs +NF/NT), Creativity (~STJ/STP vs +NF/NT), Energy (+STJ/STP vs +NF/NT)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Coercive authority in a psychological-safety-dependent environment. NF/SFP requires psychological safety for authentic creative expression. STJ/STP installs coercive authority through demonstrated force. The first visible consequence in an NF/SFP environment — for non-compliance, failure, or challenge — eliminates the psychological safety that NF/SFP's creative output requires.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Authenticity versus tactical cunning. NF/SFP's only currency is authentic expression. STJ/STP's primary tool is tactical cunning. STJ/STP managers recognise NF/SFP's authenticity signal as exploitable: NF/SFP participants who express authentic vulnerability are read by STJ/STP logic as demonstrating weakness.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Complete creative collapse. NF/SFP creative output depends on conditions STJ/STP eliminates by structural definition: safety to fail, freedom to express authentically, absence of punitive authority. By Month 9, the creative output that justified the acquisition has degraded completely.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe the first public penalisation event by STJ/STP management in the NF/SFP target. NF/SFP participants' response to the first visible consequence reveals whether psychological safety has already been eliminated.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (-STJ/STP vs +NF/SFP), Health (~STJ/STP vs +NF/SFP), Creativity (~STJ/STP vs +NF/SFP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Community versus coercion retention. SFJ/SFP retains people through genuine relational bonds — leaving feels like betrayal. STJ/STP retains people through fear and demonstrated power. STJ/STP acquirers cannot replicate SFJ/SFP's relational retention mechanism, but their coercive retention attempt destroys the relational bonds that were holding people. The community dissolves; the coercive replacement is perceived as alien; people leave.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Innovation threat amplification. SFJ/SFP already resists innovation unless framed as tradition. STJ/STP's tactical-innovation-only stance compounds this — STJ/STP resists structural innovation because it threatens existing power. The combined effect: an organisation that resists both strategic and cultural change with two reinforcing mechanisms.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Warmth extraction by coercive logic. SFJ/SFP's warmth, care, and belonging are genuine structural features — not performance. STJ/STP exploits these structures: the warmth that SFJ/SFP provides becomes a mechanism for managing compliance rather than for genuine care. SFJ/SFP participants experience the transition as a betrayal of the community's fundamental nature, which it is.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether SFJ/SFP's long-tenure insiders — those who constitute the relational network's core — begin meeting informally without STJ/STP management present. Informal community assembly outside formal structure is the SFJ/SFP resistance signal — the relational network is attempting to preserve itself against coercive intrusion.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (+SFJ/SFP vs −STJ/STP), Health (+SFJ/SFP vs ~STJ/STP), Connections (+SFJ/SFP vs ~STJ/STP)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Force versus sacred narrative as compliance mechanism. Both STJ/STP and NF/SFJ extract resources upward — but through different compliance mechanisms. STJ/STP uses force; NF/SFJ uses belief. STJ/STP acquirers dismantling the belief mechanism while installing force produces a compliance vacuum: NF/SFJ participants selected for belief, not force tolerance, cannot sustain the new authority.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Narrative authority elimination. NF/SFJ authority belongs to those most associated with the sacred narrative. STJ/STP authority belongs to whoever projects the most strength. STJ/STP displaces narrative authority with force authority, which NF/SFJ participants do not recognise as legitimate. Those who believed most deeply exit; those who remain adapt to force-based compliance.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Belief community conversion to coercion. The transition produces an environment retaining NF/SFJ's surface language (mission, values, belonging) while operating through STJ/STP's force-based compliance underneath. Structurally stable but more exploitative than either source environment.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether NF/SFJ's mission-language communications continue under STJ/STP governance — and whether they are backed by force-based compliance or genuine belief management. Mission language backed by visible consequences for non-compliance signals STJ/STP conversion of NF/SFJ.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (-STJ/STP vs -NF/SFJ — both suppress), Energy (+STJ/STP vs -NF/SFJ)

frictionLookup.fp1PrimaryFriction: Coercive redistribution versus engineered desire extraction. Both extract resources upward but through completely different mechanisms. STJ/STP takes from those who cannot defend themselves through force. SFP/SFJ extracts through manufactured desire and structural dependency. STJ/STP acquirers cannot operate SFP/SFJ's engineered desire system — the force mechanism cannot replicate desire-engineering.
frictionLookup.fp2SecondaryFriction: Compliance mechanism displacement. SFP/SFJ compliance operates through manufactured desire and financial dependency. STJ/STP compliance operates through fear. Replacing desire-compliance with force-compliance changes the participant profile required — the high-turnover, desire-responsive workforce SFP/SFJ has selected for is not a force-responsive workforce.
frictionLookup.fp3TertiaryFrictionDepartureTrigger: Extraction system collapse. STJ/STP dismantling SFP/SFJ's commercial infrastructure in favour of force-based extraction produces neither a functional SFP/SFJ nor a functional STJ/STP. The commercial system that generated the target's value is destroyed before the coercive system can generate equivalent returns.
frictionLookup.earlyWarningSignal: Within 30 days: observe whether STJ/STP management maintains or eliminates SFP/SFJ's employee-facing desire-engineering mechanisms — benefits programmes, aspirational messaging, consumer product access. Elimination signals STJ/STP's force-based logic replacing the engineered desire architecture.
frictionLookup.primaryConflictedResources: Trust (-STJ/STP vs -SFP/SFJ — compound suppression), Reputation (+STJ/STP vs -SFP/SFJ)

cells.1: RISK CATEGORY TAGGING · How friction points map to canonical risk categories · v3.1

Each directed pair in the Friction_Lookup sheet is tagged with three risk categories — one per friction point (FP1 / FP2 / FP3). The 10 canonical risk categories are defined in ST_Canonical_Schema.xlsx sheet 13 and operationalised in ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx sheet 6_Risk_Output_Composition. Tagging rules are specified below.
TAGGING RULES — friction theme → canonical risk category

cells.2: When to tag
cells.3: Friction-theme indicators
cells.4: Methodology reference

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is fundamental authority-mechanism incompatibility between acquirer and target environments — i.e. the acquirer cannot exercise legitimate authority in the target's authority terms.
cells.3: "Authority legitimacy collapse", "decision-cycle friction", "authority mechanism mismatch", "fundamental authority logic conflict", "argument quality versus track record authority", "argument quality versus narrative authority"
cells.4: §6 — overallSeverity high to critical when this is FP1

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is asymmetric vs symmetric accountability — typically when one environment protects leadership from standards applied to others.
cells.3: "Accountability asymmetry", "accountability symmetry disruption", "accountability symmetry reimposition", "asymmetric accountability versus", "senior figures protected", "structural exemption"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP2; severity correlates with how visible the asymmetry is to talent

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is retention-mechanism conflict — when the acquirer cannot replicate or replace the target's retention mechanism.
cells.3: "Retention mechanism failure", "belonging replacement by belief", "relational retention replacing structural dependency", "warmth absorption", "lock-in", "departure trigger"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP3

cells.2: Tag when friction theme involves founder, sacred narrative, or doctrinal authority concentrated in one person or small group.
cells.3: "Sacred narrative", "founding mission", "doctrinal authority", "narrative authority", "heresy mechanism", "mission alignment", "belief management"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP1 when acquirer is NF/SFJ; or any pair where target has founder-narrative authority

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is contested decision authority — who decides what, when, and on what basis.
cells.3: "Decision-cycle friction", "decision logic", "authority belongs to", "documentation and process compliance", "speed versus epistemic quality", "decision authority"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP1 or FP2

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is documented governance failure or governance migration friction — formal accountability structures that cannot be migrated cleanly.
cells.3: "Governance imposition friction", "ethics mechanism", "accountability structure installation", "ethical infection", "governance migration", "compliance mechanism"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP1 or FP2

cells.2: Tag when friction theme is status-protective behaviour — protection of senior figures, asymmetric standards, exit mechanisms designed to protect the protected class.
cells.3: "Senior figures protected", "advancement based on", "force-based authority", "loyalty protection", "engineered desire", "extraction mechanism", "asymmetric accountability with upward exemption"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP1 when acquirer is STJ/STP, SFP/SFJ, or STP/STJ

cells.2: Tag when friction theme produces talent departure as the FP3 mechanism — when high-performers exit because the new environment is incompatible with what attracted them.
cells.3: "Talent departure", "talent exodus", "meritocratic talent exit", "cognitive talent exit", "creative community dissolution", "competence exit", "high-performer departure"
cells.4: §6 — typically FP3

cells.2: Tag when friction theme produces management capability degradation — typically when target managers cannot operate under acquirer governance OR acquirer managers cannot manage target operations.
cells.3: "Management performance", "decision-making cycles slow", "capability degradation", "managerial paralysis", "throughput reduction", "creative output collapse", "knowledge attrition"
cells.4: §6 — FP3 when management capability is the lost asset

cells.2: Reserve for aggregate Risk Output composition. Do NOT tag at the individual pair level. Used at engagement aggregation when ≥3 of the per-pair tertiary categories combine into projected 6-18 month failure.
cells.3: Aggregated downstream — not used in this lookup table
cells.4: §6 — RiskOutput composite, not friction-point individual tag

cells.1: AGGREGATION LOGIC — engagement composite from per-pair tags

When the engagement's acquirer-target environment pair is identified, the analyst reads the row from Friction_Lookup and obtains: (1) FP1 risk category — typically the dominant Day 30-60 risk; (2) FP2 risk category — Months 2-6 secondary risk; (3) FP3 risk category — Months 6-18 departure-trigger risk. These three become the per-engagement starting anchors for Risk Output composition (per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §6). The analyst then composes additional Analyst Assessment records in other risk categories based on engagement-specific evidence (cross-respondent contradictions, document divergence, etc.). The pair-tag is the floor — not the ceiling — of risk categories the engagement may produce.

cells.2: Step 15.5 — after CLIENT_OUTPUT is assembled in ST_Step3_Enhanced_Output_v1.xlsx and BEFORE delivering to the client. Also used at Month 6 and Month 12 post-close verification checkpoints. Also used after N>=5 verified cases to trigger calibration updates.

1. Open ST_Transition_Rate_Engine_v1.xlsx PREDICTION_REGISTER. Confirm all 10 columns of the deal row are complete. 2. Copy the row (values only — not formulas). 3. Open SEALED_PREDICTIONS sheet of this file. Append the row to the next empty row. 4. Return to ST_Transition_Rate_Engine_v1.xlsx PREDICTION_REGISTER. Enter timestamp in col I. Set col J = SEALED. 5. Only after status = SEALED: deliver CLIENT_OUTPUT to client.

Contact client or review available data: was the predicted signal code observed in the predicted context within the predicted week window? Open VERIFICATION_LOG. Add one row: Deal ID, Ni1 (or relevant function), ECS pair, Month 6, Prediction 1. Enter actual outcome in plain language. Select outcome code from dropdown: CONFIRMED / PARTIAL / FALSIFIED / INSUFFICIENT_DATA.

Has a category transition been observed? If yes: enter actual months elapsed in Actual TR column. If no and prediction window has not yet been reached: enter PENDING. If no and prediction window has passed: enter FALSIFIED and document actual state.

Have key personnel departures occurred in the predicted window? Count senior departures (VP level and above). Enter outcome in VERIFICATION_LOG. If CONFIRMED or PARTIAL: add a row to CALIBRATION_LOG for Prediction 2.

Filter CALIBRATION_LOG for the relevant Dom.Function + Transition category. Compute average of Actual TR column (excluding PENDING rows). Open ST_Transition_Rate_Engine_v1.xlsx BASE_RATES. Replace the yellow prior value with the computed average. Change cell background to green. Return to CALIBRATION_LOG: update N_confirmed counter in STATUS TABLE. Enter YES in Update_Applied column. Update ACCURACY_DASHBOARD function row with new N counts.

All N=5 actions plus: open ST_Transition_Rate_Engine_v1.xlsx CALC_OUTPUT. For each actor row where Dom.Function matches the calibrated function: change CB Lower coefficient from 0.75 to 0.85 and CB Upper from 1.25 to 1.15 in the formula. Document the update in CALIBRATION_LOG with date and contributing deal IDs.

After every verification round: manually update the per-function accuracy table. Increment N_verified, N_confirmed, N_partial, or N_falsified as appropriate. The CB_accuracy % formula auto-calculates. Update overall stats section with aggregate figures. This sheet is the primary exhibit for institutional client credibility — keep it current.

If an error is found in a sealed prediction row: do not delete or modify the row. Add a correction note in the rightmost column of the SEALED_PREDICTIONS row. Document in VERIFICATION_LOG with outcome = FALSIFIED and note: 'prediction contained error — see note'. The integrity of the ledger requires that all errors are visible and documented, not hidden.

Do not modify SEALED_PREDICTIONS rows after Status = SEALED. Do not paste formulas into SEALED_PREDICTIONS — values only. Do not deliver Step 3 output without first creating a SEALED_PREDICTIONS entry. Do not update BASE_RATES before N=5 threshold is reached. Do not show client-identifying information in ACCURACY_DASHBOARD when sharing externally.

A sealed prediction may be created only by the engagement's Lead Analyst with co-sign by the Senior Partner per ST_Analyst_Methodology.xlsx §9. No other role may create or modify a SEALED row. Both signatures required in Section 19 of the engagement report before the SEALED_PREDICTIONS row is added.

New information that emerges post-seal does NOT modify the SEALED_PREDICTIONS row. Append to a separate POST_SEAL_INFORMATION_LOG (Phase 4 work item) recording: deal ID, information date, source, content, would-have-changed-prediction assessment, decision (re-issue as new sealed row / annotate / no action). Original sealed prediction stays immutable.

PARTIAL outcome code: direction correct, magnitude off (transition occurred outside CB, departure at different rank). PARTIAL counts as 0.5 in calibration math. Implemented in ACCURACY_DASHBOARD "CB accuracy %".

FALSIFIED outcome: prediction wrong. Falsification documented WITHOUT modifying SEALED_PREDICTIONS row. Verification entry alone records outcome. Methodology review triggered if 3 of 5 verifications for any function-transition pair are FALSIFIED (per CALIBRATION_LOG row 17), but sealed predictions remain immutable.

A sealed prediction may be quoted in marketing or institutional materials ONLY after: (a) verification window closed (Month 18); (b) outcome CONFIRMED or PARTIAL (never FALSIFIED); (c) Senior Partner approves quote in writing; (d) deal-pair anonymisation verified by Legal review. ACCURACY_DASHBOARD SEALED PREDICTION SHOWCASE is canonical source for approved quotes.

Even after deal-pair anonymisation, anonymized case study requires written client approval from original engagement client. Industry-specific patterns can re-identify well-anonymised cases; methodology reputation depends on never breaching client trust. If client refuses approval, case study filed in non-public archive, cited only by ID.
Every action (seal, verify, mark partial, mark falsified, calibration update, quote approval) recorded in PROVENANCE sheet with action timestamp, actor name+role, action type, deal ID, justification.
